title: Pelican to Murder — Level 3 Junior Detective
description: Level 3 version with social-media misdirection and cross-app evidence matching.

answerKey.method: Lured Lena to the photo alcove and triggered her allergy by switching garnish oil
answerKey.motive: To stop Lena from exposing a staged charity livestream scam
answerKey.keyEvidence: Raw livestream cache, garnish bottle photo, and deleted DM thread
answerKey.explanation: Brielle used the livestream chaos to create an alibi while raw cache files placed her near the photo alcove. The garnish oil photo and deleted DMs show both the method and motive: Lena had proof that Brielle’s charity promo was staged.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Tessa Marin
contacts.text: Tessa says she wore the sash because the show required a dramatic reveal. She says the glitter found later proves she was visible, not hidden.
contacts.sight: A visible record or object detail can be compared against the suspect timeline.
contacts.timelineNote: Compare this statement against the evidence time before trusting the alibi.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Brielle’s manager is saved as “Donation Push.”
contacts.timelineNote: This detail belongs on the case timeline and should be compared against later evidence.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Owen Landry
contacts.text: Owen says he argued with Lena about money, but he claims he left because he was tired of being accused in public.
contacts.timelineNote: Compare this statement against the evidence time before trusting the alibi.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Lena saved the same number under “charity mismatch.”
contacts.timelineNote: This detail belongs on the case timeline and should be compared against later evidence.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Brielle Dupré
contacts.text: Brielle says her livestream was delayed by the venue Wi-Fi. She insists the delay makes her look suspicious, but does not prove she moved evidence.
contacts.sight: A visible record or object detail can be compared against the suspect timeline.
contacts.weather: Humidity and rain near the patio/corridor affect footprints, visibility, and movement claims.
contacts.timelineNote: Compare this statement against the evidence time before trusting the alibi.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Cole Vinet
contacts.text: Cole says he never touched the trophy table after sound check. He remembers Tessa pacing near it, but admits he was focused on audio.
contacts.sight: A visible record or object detail can be compared against the suspect timeline.
contacts.sound: The sound/fog cue timing gives players a way to place movement during the noisy moment.
contacts.physics: Fog drift and cue timing point to controlled equipment behavior, not random chaos.
contacts.timelineNote: Compare this statement against the evidence time before trusting the alibi.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Marisol Reyes
contacts.text: Marisol says Lena disliked shortcuts and fake drama. She believes Lena would have confronted someone privately before exposing them publicly.
contacts.timelineNote: Compare this statement against the evidence time before trusting the alibi.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Andre Batiste
contacts.text: Andre says the back hall mirror makes it hard to tell who turned toward the trophy table and who simply passed it.
contacts.timelineNote: Compare this statement against the evidence time before trusting the alibi.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Nina Savoie
contacts.text: Nina says Tessa wanted attention, but not disgrace. She suggests humiliation may explain Tessa’s temper better than murder.
contacts.timelineNote: Compare this statement against the evidence time before trusting the alibi.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Quinn Rabalais
contacts.text: Quinn says the prop table was reset twice before doors opened. After that, anyone with a staff badge could get close.
contacts.sight: A visible record or object detail can be compared against the suspect timeline.
contacts.weather: Humidity and rain near the patio/corridor affect footprints, visibility, and movement claims.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Pelican to Murder. You are playing Level 3: Junior Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 3: Junior Detective Case. The culprit was Brielle Hart. The method: Lured Lena to the photo alcove and triggered her allergy by switching garnish oil. The motive: To stop Lena from exposing a staged charity livestream scam. The proof: Raw livestream cache, garnish bottle photo, and deleted DM thread.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: Where was Lena lured before the collapse?
questions.roundScope: Use only the evidence, statements, sensory notes, and story times revealed in this round.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which movement clue breaks the livestream alibi?

options.label: Her device left the livestream table

options.label: The garnish oil bottle on the prop tray

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Brielle used garnish oil during the alcove handoff
Tessa keyed the wrong register code
To stop Lena exposing the charity livestream scam

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: Raw cache, deleted DMs, and garnish photo
Tessa said the glass had a marker
Brielle claimed she never left the livestream table
Cole said he reset the booth
